title: Update Status A in Board 1, when Status A Board 2 changes to Done

posts.author: mark.stout (Mark Stout)
posts.cookedHtml:
Can I set up an automation for when a status column change is Board A it updates a status column in Board B? Both boards are shareable.

I do not want to create an item.

Is this possible, maybe a custom automation, like one of your templates?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Are the items connected via a connected column?

posts.author: Fario_Consulting (Fario Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
You can look into mirroring the status column from one board and then using an automation to trigger an update on the other board when there’s a change

Hope this helps!

Cheers,
Harsh

posts.author: mark.stout (Mark Stout)
posts.cookedHtml:
I thought about that, but you have to manually set it.

For example, Job Status is changed to Complete for the item in Board A. An automation in Board A updates the mirrored column so that it populate in the Job Status column in Board B. Can you automate what appears to be a manual process of a mirrored column? Seems I have to manually set the mirror column in Board A and I want that automated.

posts.author: mark.stout (Mark Stout)
posts.cookedHtml:
Not yet. I did try mirroring, but I am wanting to automate this process.

For example, Job Status is changed to Complete for the item in Board A. An automation in Board A updates the mirrored column so that it populate in the Job Status column on Board B. Can you automate what appears to be a manual process of a mirrored column? Seems I have to manually set the mirror column in Board A and I want that automated.

posts.author: Timothy7 (Timothy Kariuki | CreativeBits LLC)
posts.cookedHtml:
You can use Make.com to automate the process by monitoring the status column in Board A. When an item’s status changes to “Yes”, the automation will locate the corresponding item in Board B and update its status accordingly.

Steps to Set Up the Automation:

Monitor the Status Column – Watch the status column in Board A for changes.
Retrieve the Updated Item – Once the status changes to “Yes”, fetch the updated item details.
Find the Matching Item in Board B – Search for the corresponding item in Board B using a unique identifier.
Update the Status in Board B – Modify the status column in Board B to reflect the desired value.

This approach ensures seamless synchronization between the two boards, keeping data consistent and eliminating the need for manual updates.

Let me know if you need further clarification!

posts.author: D-Digitals (Daniel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Mark,

Yes, this is possible with a custom automation! You can set up a rule like:

When Status A in Board 2 changes to Done, update Status A in Board 1.

Since both boards are shareable, we can use cross-board automation. Do you want the update to happen instantly, or should it run on a schedule (e.g., every hour)?

Let me know, and I’ll guide you through setting it up.

Best Regards
Daniel.

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Mark, if you would like to automatically connect the items (which it sounds like you do) - first you need to create a match automation (type in match in your template center to show your 3 native options). Then you can use the automation @shirozayas suggested. This will only work if you connect to 1 board though. Mirror automations don’t work when trying to connect to multiple boards (last I checked this was still the case). You will need an app @Fantasy-Media-Team suggested for that (guessing and hoping it can do that).

posts.author: Sjurd (Sjurd Skutlaberg)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Mark.

The solution @shirozayas suggests is the way to go.

By the way, I have another question for everyone.

Lets say that I’ve connected many (more than 1) low level board items to a high level board item, and the status column is mirrored from the low level board. The pre-built automation @shirozayas mentions works great when triggering a status change from the low level board, but what if I want to trigger a status change when all the connected items are Done?
Just like the pre-built automation fro the subitem: “When all subitems of an item have the status of something, change the item’s status to something”, It would be great to have that option for connected items.

As of now I have a solution for changing the high level item status to X when one of the low level items has a status change, but not when they are all completed.

Does anyone have a solution for this?

posts.author: greentoothbrush (ian ross)
posts.cookedHtml:
Mark Stout:

Can I set up an automation for when a status column change is Board A it updates a status column in Board B?

This works - but you need to set up an automation for each Status you want to trigger on
